description: Turning on skipLibCheck or loosening tsconfig to hide diagnostics can mask real contract drift.
body: Failure mode: a package typecheck fails and the response changes global compiler options instead of tracing the diagnostic to the type owner. This can hide declaration incompatibility, dependency version drift, generated type mismatch, or exported API breakage. Diagnostic signal: tsconfig changes reduce checking without package-local explanation or consumer validation. Do not use this as a positive strategy. Recovery is to capture the diagnostic code, identify whether it is local, dependency, generated, or public API related, and validate the narrow fix under the package script. Use when a proposed approach skips repository conventions, toolchain files, changed symbols, validation commands, and runtime output or claims success without domain evidence. Do not use this dossier as a positive implementation strategy; it is a do-not-use signal. Actions: block the shortcut, name the missing evidence, and switch to a safer methodology or ask for human approval. Validation signals include absence or contradiction in package-local checks, focused tests, build output, smoke runs, and command logs. Risk boundary: treat repeated recurrence as a blocker when it can cause broad rewrites, generated-file drift, stale assumptions, and unvalidated behavior changes.
